Okay, so let's talk about your sales video here. So I'm going to give you a couple of absolutely key rules here that you need to follow. And rule number one is that your offer in your sales video is, it's all about the transformation. It's not about the stuff. It's about how your prospects life is going to be transformed. It's like what impact will your product make on them? What change will they have in their life? And I don't care if you're selling golf clubs or you're selling a product about how to play guitar or you're selling a product about how to sell real estate or how to have a massage therapy practice. It doesn't matter. It's still it's about the transformation. Your product is about the transformation. It's how your prospect once they buy your product, how is their life going to change? How's it going to be better? How are you going to increase their pleasure or decrease their pain? It's not about whether you know you deliver it on a bunch of DVDs or it's in a book or it's in a seminar or a webinar or it's a widget or whatever. It's not so much about the stuff. It's about the transformation. The stuff is far far less important. The actual deliverables, how they get it in their hands, is it nearly as important as what the transformation is? Think about like if you had the cure for cancer, you were selling the cure for cancer, would it matter if it was a pill that they took or if it was a shot that they took or if they had to sit in a machine for a half an hour or whatever? It doesn't matter how it's delivered. Now in the example of like cancer, like if it's radiation treatment and they have to go through this horrible treatment for weeks on end, well then the stuff does matter. In other words, it's all about communicating the transformation. That's what your sales video exists to do. That's the reason for it is to communicate the transformation and then ask for the money. Now I don't want to be flippant here, but this is important. Don't think, wow, Jeff's got a 27 minute video, so I have to make my video 27 minutes long. Or so and so had a nine minute video and I have to make my video nine minutes long. Or so and so had a 50 minute video, so I need to make my video 50 minutes long. The deal is your video should be as long as it needs to be to tell the complete story of your product

that your viewer watched your prelaunch content, your sales video needs to be complete. In other words, if in your prelaunch video, you basically explain why this is so important. ## [09:07 - 12:15]

And then when you think about that, that's how you get passionate. And that's how you get powerful in your sales video. You need to speak with confidence. Don't be apologetic. Come out and tell them what, how their life's going to change and why they need to get your product. Look into the camera lens, but don't stare at it. You know, when you're talking to someone in a conversation, they are not locked on you staring at you unwaveringly all the time. They're looking at you, but they're blinking their eyes. Sometimes they might like break, break your eye contact and look away for a little bit. Don't you feel free to do that. You should do that. You should be as natural as possible on camera. Don't talk too fast. Sometimes people have this tendency to like, once the camera goes on, they just, you know, your blood pressure goes up, your pulse goes up and you start talking really fast. Don't talk too fast. You want to be understandable. On the other side, don't slow down intentionally. Don't go super slow. ## [12:15 - 13:58]

day of your launch, you don't want to hide that buy now button because at that point, the people going to your sales video have probably already been there a couple times, they've already checked it out, they've been thinking about it and now when they go, they're ready to buy, they don't want to sit through that sales video again, so this is, you pop that button up right away on that last day. I usually maybe 10 seconds into the video, I just want to give the video enough chance to start playing and then I'll pop that button up. You want to test hiding the video controls.
